mnemonics: Greedy Goes for the Goal: Uses only the heuristic to choose the next node, always going for what looks closest to the goal.
keyPoints: It is an informed search algorithm that uses a heuristic function h(n) to estimate the cost to the goal from node n.
It expands the node that appears to be closest to the goal, based solely on the heuristic value, making it 'greedy'.
It is not optimal and can be misled by an inaccurate heuristic, potentially resulting in inefficient paths or getting stuck in loops.
commonMistakes: Confusing it with A* search, which uses both the cost to reach the node (g(n)) and the heuristic (h(n)), not just the heuristic.
Assuming it is complete; while it may find a solution in finite state spaces with cycle checking, it is not guaranteed to find a solution in all cases, especially with an poor heuristic.
